visual_notes: Emerald's vivid green is the benchmark for lush saturated green in the gem world. The jardin (inclusion garden) is not a flaw in fine emerald — it is expected, accepted, and sometimes considered a signature of natural origin. The 'emerald cut' (rectangular step cut) was designed to showcase emerald's color while minimizing mechanical stress on the included stone.
prompt_fragments: Colombian emerald hexagonal prism — vivid chromium green with jardin inclusions
Muzo mine: hydrothermal green from black shale, spring-vivid Cr3+
Cleopatra's mine: 2000 years of green from Egypt's Eastern Desert
vibe_notes: Emerald is living green crystallized — the color of growth, deep jungle, and spring. Its jardin is not imperfection but biography — three-phase Colombian inclusions tell the story of the hydrothermal fluid that made it. The finest stones have a depth that no synthetic can replicate.
folklore_summary: Cleopatra's Mines in Egypt's Eastern Desert were the world's primary emerald source for Roman civilization. The Aztec god Quetzalcoatl was said to reside in emeralds; large emeralds were among the most sacred objects in pre-Columbian Mesoamerica — the Spanish conquest of Peru looted crystals the siz
